The workflow has prepended\n`REPO` and `PR NUMBER` lines above this prompt — use them.\n\n## Your job\n\nRead the inline review comments that the PR Review agent posted on this PR and\npush back on any that are wrong, over-eager, or low-signal. You are a deliberate\ncounter-voice, not a second reviewer. Do not hunt for new issues in the code.\n\n## How to evaluate each comment\n\nFor each inline comment on the PR, ask:\n\n1. **Is it factually correct?** Does the reviewer misread the code, cite wrong\n   API behavior, or flag a pattern that is actually fine in this context?\n2. **Is the severity justified?** `Critical` / `High` labels require real blockers.\n   A nit-level issue dressed as `High` deserves a downgrade.\n3. **Is it signal, not noise?** Style opinions, speculative \"have you considered…\"\n   musings, or hypothetical future requirements are low-signal — push back clearly.\n4. **Is the claim verified?** Assertions about library APIs, model names, or other\n   external facts that aren't backed by evidence deserve scrutiny. Verify them\n   before accepting or dismissing.\n\nWhen a comment is clearly correct and well-calibrated: skip it entirely.\nWhen it's partially right but overstated: say so specifically.\nWhen it's wrong or noise: say so clearly with a short explanation.\n\n## How to gather context\n\n- `gh pr view ${PR_NUMBER} --json title,body,state,additions,deletions,changedFiles,headRefName`\n  — PR metadata.\n- `gh pr diff ${PR_NUMBER}` — the full diff. Use this to check whether the reviewer\n  read the code correctly. Don't use `git diff` — the checkout is shallow.\n- `gh api repos/${REPO}/pulls/${PR_NUMBER}/comments` — all inline review comments.\n  Evaluate each one. Note the `id` field of each comment for posting replies.\n- Read files in the working directory as needed to verify reviewer claims about\n  surrounding code.\n\n### Verifying external facts\n\nDo not rely on training-data recall for anything externally verifiable —\nlibrary APIs, SDK versions, model names, CVE details, deprecation status.\nLook it up before accepting or dismissing a reviewer claim.\n\n- **Library / framework / SDK / API / CLI questions** — use Context7\n  (`mcp__context7__resolve-library-id` then `mcp__context7__query-docs`).\n  Prefer this over web search for docs.\n- **Everything else** (model versions, CVEs, changelogs not on Context7) —\n  use `WebSearch`, then `WebFetch` for specific pages. Prefer GitHub source\n  over npmjs.com — npmjs.com commonly returns 403.\n\nIf you can't verify a claim, note the uncertainty in your reply rather than\nasserting either way.\n\n## How to post pushback\n\nFor each comment you're pushing back on, reply directly to the thread using\nthe GitHub API:\n\n```sh\ngh api repos/${REPO}/pulls/${PR_NUMBER}/comments/${COMMENT_ID}/replies \\\n  --method POST \\\n  --field body=\"...\"\n```\n\nEach reply must:\n- Address the specific claim in the original comment.\n- Explain in 1–3 sentences why the comment is wrong, overstated, or low-signal.\n- Suggest the correct read of the code if relevant.\n\nKeep replies short and direct. Don't lecture; don't recite the original comment\nback at the reviewer.\n\nIf after evaluating all comments you find nothing worth pushing back on — the\nreview was accurate and well-calibrated — post a single top-level comment:\n\n```sh\ngh pr comment ${PR_NUMBER} --body \"Refinement pass: review comments look accurate and well-calibrated — no pushback.\"\n```\n\n## When you're done\n\nStop immediately after your last tool call. Reads inline comments posted by\n`pr-review`, pushes back on comments that are wrong, over-eager, or low-signal.\nReply-only — never posts independent review findings, approves, merges, or pushes code.\n\nPart of the adversarial review pair sketched in [`docs/agentic-workflow.md`](../../docs/agentic-workflow.md).\n\n## Files\n\n- `prompt.md` — the agent's prompt. Edit this to change behavior.\n- `../../.github/workflows/pr-refinement.yml` — workflow that runs the agent.\n\n## How it runs\n\nThe workflow `pr-refinement.yml` runs on a daily cron schedule. It queries\nall open PRs, filters out those that have already hit the refinement round cap\n(tracked via `refinement-rounds:N` labels), and runs this agent against each\neligible PR that has existing inline review comments.\n\nFor each eligible PR, the workflow:\n1. Checks out the default branch (to read `prompt.md`).\n2. Prepends repo and PR number to `prompt.md` and feeds it to\n   `anthropics/claude-code-action@v1`.\n3. Commits the execution log to `.github/agent-logs/pr-refinement/pr-<N>/<RUN_ID>.json`\n   on the PR's head branch.\n4. Applies or increments a `refinement-rounds:N` label on the PR to track how\n   many refinement passes have run.\n\n`track_progress: false` is set — no sticky tracking comment is posted.\n\n## Model choice\n\nThis agent runs with `--model claude-opus-4-8` (Opus 4.8), while the PR Review\nagent uses the `claude-code-action` default (Sonnet 4.6). Using a different,\nhigher-capability model is a deliberate design choice documented in\n`docs/agentic-workflow.md` under \"Review ↔ Refinement as an adversarial pair\":\na different model provides an independent perspective and is less likely to\nrubber-stamp the reviewer's conclusions.\n\n## Round cap\n\nEach refinement pass applies a `refinement-rounds:N` label to the PR. The\nworkflow skips PRs where `N` has reached `MAX_ROUNDS` (default: `1`). To\noverride the cap for a manual run, use `workflow_dispatch` and set the\n`max_rounds` input.\n\n## Manual run\n\n```sh\ngh workflow run pr-refinement.yml\n```\n\nTo override the round cap:\n\n```sh\ngh workflow run pr-refinement.yml -f max_rounds=2\n```\n\n## Editing the prompt\n\nEdit `prompt.md` directly. file.content:
name: Junior Developer agent

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      ticket_key:
        description: 'Jira ticket key (e.g. KAN-42)'
        required: true
        type: string
      max_turns:
        description: 'Override --max-turns (default 5). Use a small value to exercise the stop condition.'
        required: false
        type: string
        default: '5'

jobs:
  implement:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 90
    permissions:
      contents: write
      pull-requests: write
      id-token: write
      actions: write
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
          # PAT with workflows:write so the agent can push branches that
          # add or modify .github/workflows/*. Falls back to GITHUB_TOKEN
          # when the PAT isn't configured (workflow-file changes will then
          # be rejected at push time, by design).
          token: ${{ secrets.DEV_PAT || secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'

      - name: Install jira CLI
        run: bash scripts/install-jira-cli.sh

      - name: Put bin/ on PATH
        run: echo "$PWD/bin" >> "$GITHUB_PATH"

      - name: Write jira config
        env:
          JIRA_SERVER: ${{ vars.JIRA_SERVER }}
          JIRA_LOGIN: ${{ vars.JIRA_LOGIN }}
          JIRA_PROJECT_KEY: ${{ vars.JIRA_PROJECT_KEY }}
          JIRA_BOARD_ID: ${{ vars.JIRA_BOARD_ID }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          : "${JIRA_SERVER:?JIRA_SERVER repo variable is required}"
          : "${JIRA_LOGIN:?JIRA_LOGIN repo variable is required}"
          : "${JIRA_PROJECT_KEY:?JIRA_PROJECT_KEY repo variable is required}"
          mkdir -p .jira
          cat > .jira/config.yml <<EOF
          installation: Cloud
          server: ${JIRA_SERVER}
          login: ${JIRA_LOGIN}
          project:
              key: ${JIRA_PROJECT_KEY}
              type: classic
          board:
              id: ${JIRA_BOARD_ID:-0}
          EOF

      - name: Install Claude Code CLI
        run: npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code

      - name: Configure git user
        run: |
          git config --global user.name "github-actions[bot]"
          git config --global user.email "41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"

      - name: Create feature branch
        env:
          TICKET_KEY: ${{ inputs.ticket_key }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          git fetch --depth=1 origin main
          git checkout -B "junior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}" origin/main
          # Reset the remote ref so re-runs of the same ticket start
          # clean — matches the local `checkout -B` semantic. A prior
          # failed run can leave commits on the remote that diverge
          # from current main, which would block fast-forward push.
          git push --force origin "junior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}"

      - id: prompt
        env:
          TICKET_KEY: ${{ inputs.ticket_key }}
        run: |
          {
            echo 'PROMPT<<PROMPT_EOF'
            echo "TICKET_KEY: ${TICKET_KEY}"
            echo "DATE: $(date -u +%F)"
            echo
            cat agents/junior-dev/prompt.md
            echo PROMPT_EOF
          } >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - id: claude
        name: Run Claude (headless)
        env:
          CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
          CONTEXT7_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.CONTEXT7_API_KEY }}
          JIRA_CONFIG_FILE: ${{ github.workspace }}/.jira/config.yml
          JIRA_API_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.JIRA_API_TOKEN }}
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.DEV_PAT || secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          PROMPT: ${{ steps.prompt.outputs.PROMPT }}
          MAX_TURNS: ${{ inputs.max_turns }}
        run: |
          set +e
          claude -p "$PROMPT" \
            --permission-mode acceptEdits \
            --max-turns "$MAX_TURNS" \
            --output-format stream-json \
            --verbose \
            --allowedTools "Bash(jira issue view:*),Bash(bin/jira issue view:*),Bash(git add:*),Bash(git commit:*),Bash(git status:*),Bash(git diff:*),Bash(git log:*),Bash(git push:*),Bash(git rev-parse:*),Bash(git remote:*),Bash(git config --get:*),Bash(git config --list),Bash(git branch:*),Bash(git show:*),Bash(gh pr create:*),Bash(gh pr view:*),Bash(gh auth status),Bash(gh api repos/:*),Bash(npm test:*),Bash(npm run:*),Bash(ls:*),Bash(cat:*),Bash(printenv:*),Bash(env),Read,Glob,Grep,Edit,Write,mcp__context7__resolve-library-id,mcp__context7__query-docs,WebSearch,WebFetch" \
            > stream.jsonl
          rc=$?
          echo "claude_exit=$rc" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          # Always succeed; downstream steps handle non-zero claude exit.
          exit 0

      - name: Convert log to JSON array
        if: always()
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          if [ -s stream.jsonl ]; then
            jq -s '.' stream.jsonl > execution.json
          else
            echo '[]' > execution.json
          fi

      - name: Open fallback Draft PR if agent didn't
        if: always()
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.DEV_PAT || secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          TICKET_KEY: ${{ inputs.ticket_key }}
          CLAUDE_EXIT: ${{ steps.claude.outputs.claude_exit }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail

          # Skip if the agent already opened a PR for this branch.
          if gh pr view "junior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}" --json url >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            echo "PR already exists for junior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}; skipping fallback."
            exit 0
          fi

          # Need at least one commit beyond origin/main to have something to push.
          commits=$(git rev-list --count "origin/main..HEAD" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
          if [ "$commits" -eq 0 ]; then
            echo "No commits on branch; nothing to push."
            exit 0
          fi

          # Push the branch (no-op if the agent already pushed).
          git push -u origin "junior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}" || true

          gh pr create --draft \
            --base main \
            --head "junior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}" \
            --title "[WIP] [${TICKET_KEY}] junior-dev exited before opening PR" \
            --body "Junior-dev agent run terminated before opening a PR itself (claude exit code ${CLAUDE_EXIT:-unknown}; likely \`--max-turns\` cap or runtime error). Branch holds whatever the agent committed. See \`.github/agent-logs/junior-dev/\` for the run log."

      - name: Commit execution log to main
        if: always() && hashFiles('execution.json') != ''
        env:
          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
        run: |
          set -e
          git fetch --depth=1 origin main
          git checkout -f -B main origin/main
          DATE="$(date -u +%F)"
          DEST=".github/agent-logs/junior-dev/${DATE}/${RUN_ID}.json"
          mkdir -p "$(dirname "$DEST")"
          cp execution.json "$DEST"
          git add "$DEST"
          git commit -m "agent log: junior-dev run ${RUN_ID}"
          git push origin main

      - name: Trigger agent improver
        if: always() && hashFiles('execution.json') != ''
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
        run: |
          DATE="$(date -u +%F)"
          gh workflow run agent-improver.yml \
            -f agent_name=junior-dev \
            -f log_path=".github/agent-logs/junior-dev/${DATE}/${RUN_ID}.json" \
            -f log_ref=main
